inclusions: Cane or beet sugar and chemically pure sucrose, in solid form
Raw sugar (polarimeter reading less than 99.5° in dry state)
Refined sugar (sucrose content corresponds to polarimeter reading of 99.5° or more)
Sugar with added flavouring or colouring matter
Other sugars including chemically pure lactose, maltose, glucose and fructose, in solid form
Sugar syrups not containing added flavouring or colouring matter
Artificial honey, whether or not mixed with natural honey
Palm sugar including coconut sap sugar
Molasses resulting from the extraction or refining of sugar
Sugar confectionery (including white chocolate), not containing cocoa
Chewing gum, whether or not sugar-coated

exclusions.condition: Chemically pure sugars other than sucrose, lactose, maltose, glucose and fructose
Medicaments or other products of Chapter 30

classification_rules.description: Raw sugar means sugar whose content of sucrose by weight, in the dry state, corresponds to a polarimeter reading of LESS THAN 99.5°.

Subheading 1701.13 covers ONLY cane sugar obtained WITHOUT centrifugation, with sucrose content corresponding to a polarimeter reading of 69° or more but less than 93°. The product contains only natural anhedral microcrystals of irregular shape, not visible to the naked eye, surrounded by residues of molasses.

Glucose and glucose syrup are split by fructose content: (a) less than 20% fructose → 1702.30; (b) 20% to less than 50% fructose (excl. invert sugar) → 1702.40; (c) more than 50% fructose (excl. invert sugar) → 1702.60.
White chocolate does NOT contain cocoa, so it falls under 1704.90 (sugar confectionery not containing cocoa), NOT under 18.06.
Medicated pastilles and drops (throat lozenges, cough drops) are classified under 1704.90.10, NOT under Chapter 30, as they are primarily confectionery products.
